import re
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from nltk.probability import FreqDist

def task_func(example_str, top_n=30):
    # Remove words enclosed in square brackets
    cleaned_str = re.sub(r'\[.*?\]', '', example_str)

    # Create a frequency distribution of the words
    freqdist = FreqDist(cleaned_str.split())

    # Plot the frequency distribution
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()
    ax.bar(*zip(*sorted(freqdist.items(), key=lambda item: item[1], reverse=True)[:top_n + 1]))
    ax.set_xlabel('Words')
    ax.set_ylabel('Frequency')
    plt.title('Word Frequency Distribution')
    plt.xticks(range(len(freqdist)), [word for word, _ in freqdist.items()], rotation=90)

    # Return the top_n most common words and their frequencies as a dictionary
    common_words = dict(sorted(freqdist.items(), key=lambda item: item[1], reverse=True)[:top_n])
    return plt.gca(), common_words
